title: FG DECLARES JUNE 12 PUBLIC HOLIDAY FOR DEMOCRACY DAY
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary:
The Federal Government has declared Friday, June 12, 2026, a public holiday to mark this year's Democracy Day celebration.
The Minister of Interior said the day is dedicated to reflecting on Nigeria's democratic journey and honouring those who contributed to the restoration of democratic governance.
Democracy Day is observed annually on June 12 in remembrance of the historic 1993 presidential election, regarded as one of Nigeria's freest and fairest polls.
Government offices, schools, banks, and many private institutions are expected to observe the holiday nationwide.
fullContent:
The Federal Government has officially declared Friday, June 12, 2026, a public holiday to commemorate this year's Democracy Day celebration across Nigeria.
The announcement was made by the Minister of Interior, who described the occasion as an important reminder of Nigeria's democratic journey and the sacrifices made by individuals and groups who fought for the restoration and sustenance of democratic governance in the country.
According to the minister, Democracy Day provides an opportunity for Nigerians to reflect on the nation's progress, appreciate the gains of democracy, and renew their commitment to the principles of freedom, justice, unity, peace, and good governance.
Democracy Day is celebrated annually on June 12 in honour of the historic June 12, 1993 presidential election, widely regarded as one of the freest, fairest, and most credible elections in Nigeria's history.
The Federal Government reaffirmed its commitment to strengthening democratic institutions, promoting national development, and ensuring that democratic values continue to thrive across the country.
As part of the holiday observance, government offices, educational institutions, banks, and many private establishments are expected to suspend normal operations, while citizens participate in activities marking the significance of the day.
Nigerians have also been encouraged to use the occasion to reflect on their role in sustaining democracy and contributing to the nation's growth and development.

title: Welcome to Big Quams Media®
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary: Big Quams Media® is Nigeria's #1 free student platform — your one-stop hub for JAMB prep, Post-UTME guides, scholarships, free eBooks and live campus news.
fullContent: Big Quams Media® was founded to bridge the gap in access to quality educational resources for Nigerian students. From JAMB past questions to Post-UTME calculators and scholarship listings, everything on this platform is completely free. Join over 10,000 students already using BQM to succeed in their academic journey.

title: JAMB 2026 UTME Results Released — Check Yours Now
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary: JAMB has officially released the 2026 UTME results. Log in to the JAMB eFacility portal to check your score and admission status on CAPS.
fullContent: The Joint Admissions and Matriculation Board (JAMB) has released the 2026 UTME results. Students can now check their scores by visiting the JAMB eFacility portal at jamb.gov.ng/efacility. You will need your registered email and JAMB registration number. After checking your result, proceed to CAPS to accept or reject your admission offer. Contact BQM via WhatsApp if you need guidance.

title: MTN Foundation Scholarship 2026 — Applications Open
author: BIG QUAMS MEDIA
summary: The MTN Foundation Scholarship for undergraduate students is now open. Eligible students in STEM and Social Sciences can apply before the deadline.
fullContent: The MTN Foundation Scholarship 2026 is now accepting applications from undergraduate students in Nigerian universities. The scholarship covers tuition and provides a stipend for living expenses. Eligible candidates must be studying STEM or Social Sciences, have a minimum CGPA of 3.5 on a 5.0 scale, and be in their 200 or 300 level. Visit the MTN Foundation website to apply before the closing date.
